plan_metadata.name: Sample Teams Conversation Bot using Python Only Remote

steps.description: Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace item in the Explorer panel within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "View Samples" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Click on the search bar in the "Samples" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface to input or filter items.
Type text: 'Teams Conversation Bot using Python' into the search bar within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Teams Conversation Bot using Python" sample card in the "Samples" gallery section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, located within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Create" button located in the "Teams Conversation Bot" sample section within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Default Folder" dropdown field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project root directory.
@assertion 'Preview README.MD' is visible
@assertion the page title contains 'Teams Conversation Bot'
Right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code editor to access context menu options for the file.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu of the tab bar to close the active file in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, within the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension interface.
Type 'python: create environment' into the Command Palette text box in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Python: Create Environment" option from the dropdown list in the command palette at coordinates (271, 57).
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of "Venv: Creates a '.venv' virtual environment in the current workspace" from the dropdown in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Python 3.12.12 64-bit" option in the dropdown for selecting the Python interpreter within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Toggle all checkboxes" button in the dependency installation dialog at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection in the "Select dependencies to install" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Type the text "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" into the command palette search bar at the top-center of the Visual Studio Code interface, shown over the application's main content.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the selected command, "Debug: Select and Start Debugging," in Visual Studio Code's Command Palette.
Click on the "Debug (Chrome)" option in the dropdown menu under the Debug configuration selector in Visual Studio Code.
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm the selected resource group within the Visual Studio Code dropdown labeled "Select a resource group."
Press the 'Enter' key to confirm input in the "New resource group name" text field within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press "Enter" to confirm the selection of "Central US" as the location for the new resource group in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents extension.
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog to proceed with provisioning resources using the listed Azure and Microsoft 365 accounts.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft account login interface.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the 'Email, phone, or Skype' input field on the Microsoft login page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email input for the "Sign in" form on the Microsoft login page.
Type the password ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page at login.microsoftonline.com.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password in the Microsoft login form.
Click the "Yes" button in the Microsoft login prompt to confirm staying signed in.
Click the "Add" button in the pop-up window for the TeamsConversationBot app on the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog of the TeamsConversationBot interface within Microsoft Teams.
Click the "View prompts" button in the TeamsConversationBot chat interface to access prompt suggestions.
Click on the "MentionMe" option under "Prompt Suggestions from TeamsConversationBot" to send a message with @mention of the sender.
Press the "Enter" key to send the message "MentionMe" in the chat input field within the TeamsConversationBot interface of Microsoft Teams (web version).
@assertion the bot replies ‘Hello’ to a test user.
Click the "View prompts" link under "Prompt Suggestions from TeamsConversationBot" in the chat area of Microsoft Teams.
Click "Show Welcome" in the "Prompt Suggestions" menu to display the welcome card in the TeamsConversationBot interface on Microsoft Teams.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the text "Show Welcome" in the chat input field of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
@assertion the bot responds with the welcome card containing the action buttons.
Click the "View prompts" button in the Microsoft Teams bot interface, located at the bottom-right corner of the chat window.
Click the "MessageAllMembers" option in the prompt suggestions panel below "Prompt Suggestions from TeamsConversationBot".
Press the 'Enter' key to send the typed command "MessageAllMembers" in the TeamsConversationBot chat interface within Microsoft Teams.
@assertion the bot replies with the expected greeting message
